title: elaeocarpus teysmannii subsp. moluccensis
intro: Elaeocarpus teysmannii subsp. moluccensis, also known as the Moluccan Blue Quince, is a plant species in the Elaeocarpus genus, native to the Moluccas Islands in Indonesia. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly for its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, which are derived from its extracts and compounds. The health benefits of Elaeocarpus teysmannii subsp. moluccensis include its potential to treat wounds, reduce fever, and alleviate respiratory issues. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used to make teas, infusions, and ointments, which are applied topically or ingested to harness its medicinal properties.
